channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇷🇴🇷🇺 Russian drone hits Romanian apartment block, NATO and EU condemn
item.description: A Russian drone targeting Ukraine struck a residential building in Romania, injuring two people. Romanian air defenses hit the drone over Ukraine, altering its trajectory. NATO and EU condemned Russia.

item.title: 🇮🇱⛪ Israel added to UN sexual violence blacklist for first time
item.description: The UN Secretary-General's annual report added Israel to the blacklist of parties credibly suspected of committing conflict-related sexual violence. Israel rejected the allegations.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump holds meeting on Iran deal but no final agreement announced
item.description: US President Trump met with advisers to make a "final determination" on a potential Iran deal. Officials confirmed a framework was agreed, but deep mistrust remains between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: 🇬🇭 Ghana parliament passes strict anti-LGBTQ+ bill
item.description: Ghana's parliament passed a bill making same-sex acts punishable by jail terms. The legislation targets those identifying as gay, lesbian, or transgender.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇭🇺 EU to release billions in frozen funds for Hungary amid reforms
item.description: The EU agreed to release billions in frozen funds after Hungary's new government under PM Peter Magyar implemented reforms. Magyar called it a "historic" breakthrough.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Strikes Oil Infrastructure in Western Russia and Occupied Crimea
item.description: Ukrainian forces reportedly struck oil infrastructure targets across multiple locations overnight, including in western Russia and Russian-occupied Crimea, according to Russian Telegram channels.

item.title: 🇯🇵🇺🇦 Japan Purchases Million in Military Equipment for Ukraine Under NATO Program
item.description: Japan is contributing non-lethal military aid to Ukraine through NATO's Prioritized Ukraine Requirements List (PURL) initiative, marking a significant commitment to Ukrainian defense support.

item.title: 🇺🇳 UN Blacklists Russian Forces Over Sexual Violence Against Ukrainian POWs
item.description: A new UN report adds Russian forces to the sexual violence "blacklist" for crimes against Ukrainian prisoners of war and civilian detainees, documenting nearly 10,000 cases of conflict-related sexual violence worldwide.

item.title: 🇦🇪🇮🇷 UAE Conducted Dozens of Airstrikes on Iran Using US and Israeli Intelligence
item.description: The Wall Street Journal reports that the UAE carried out multiple strikes on Iranian targets, including the Asaluyeh petrochemical complex, using intelligence provided by the US and Israel during the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanese Army Unable to Disarm Hezbollah, Israeli Army Sources Say
item.description: Israeli military officials assess that the Lebanese Armed Forces lack the capability to disarm Hezbollah, raising concerns about the implementation of any ceasefire agreement in Lebanon.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇱 US Has Almost Completely Excluded Israel From Iran Negotiations
item.description: Israeli defense officials tell The Times of Israel that Washington is conducting talks with Tehran without meaningful Israeli involvement, raising concerns in Tel Aviv about the direction of diplomatic efforts.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 IRGC-Trained Operative Plotted to Assassinate Ivanka Trump
item.description: An alleged Iran-linked operative planned to assassinate Ivanka Trump in retaliation for the 2020 US killing of Iranian Qasem Soleimani, according to a report by Al Arabiya.

item.title: 🇹🇷⚔️ Turkish Intelligence Captures 10 ISIS Suspects in Syria Operation
item.description: Turkiye's MIT and Syrian intelligence conducted a joint operation capturing 10 ISIS suspects, including one linked to the Ankara train station attack.

item.title: 🇺🇦💥 Russian Drones Kill 5, Injure 62 Across Ukraine
item.description: Russian drone attacks killed 5 people and injured 62 across Ukraine, including strikes on homes and a church in Kherson. A 69-year-old cyclist was hit by an FPV drone.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine trains elite drone pilots as electronic warfare intensifies
item.description: Ukrainian military pilots demonstrated interceptor drone skills in western Ukraine, training to counter Russian drone threats as electronic warfare becomes increasingly critical to the conflict.

item.title: 🦠 Uganda confirms three new Ebola cases as outbreak spreads
item.description: Uganda reported three new Ebola cases on Saturday, bringing the total confirmed cases to five. The WHO has warned against underestimating the risk of regional spread from the DRC-Uganda outbreak.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey detains 13 in probe of opposition congress that chose ousted leader
item.description: Turkish police detained 13 people in an investigation into a 2023 opposition CHP congress that elected the mayor of Istanbul, as political tensions continue in Turkey.

item.title: ⚔️ Death toll in Luhansk student dorm strike rises to 10
item.description: A Russian-installed official said the death toll from a drone strike on a student dormitory in the Russian-controlled Luhansk region of eastern Ukraine has risen to 10.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇺🇦 Putin vows retaliation after accusing Ukraine of deadly dormitory attack
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin promised revenge after accusing Ukraine of carrying out a deadly attack on a student dormitory in Russian-controlled Luhansk that he says killed six people. Ukraine dismissed the claim, stating the attack was exclusively targeted at military infrastructure.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇹🇼 US pauses $14bn arms sale to Taiwan amid China pressure
item.description: The United States has paused a major arms sale package to Taiwan worth $14 billion, according to reports. Taiwan's Premier Cho Jung-tai said the island would continue pursuing arms purchases despite the delay, which comes amid heightened US-China tensions and the ongoing Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇸🇳 Senegal President sacks PM Sonko, dissolves government
item.description: President Bassirou Diomaye Faye dismissed Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko and dissolved the government after months of tensions, deepening political uncertainty in the West African nation.

item.title: 🦠 International Contact Group urges DRC conflict parties to allow Ebola response
item.description: The International Contact Group for the Great Lakes raised concerns about continued conflict in eastern DRC hampering Ebola response efforts, as the outbreak continues to spread.

item.title: 🌐 INTERPOL arrests 201 suspects in largest cybercrime operation
item.description: INTERPOL conducted its largest cybercrime operation to date, targeting phishing, malware, and online scams across the Middle East and North Africa, resulting in 201 arrests.

item.title: 🇧🇴 Bolivian police clash with protesters in anti-government marches
item.description: Bolivian riot police clashed with anti-government protesters in La Paz for the second time in a week, as unions and opposition groups demand President Luis Arce resign.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇹🇼 US Arms Sales to Taiwan Unrelated to Iran War, Source Says
item.description: A source familiar with the matter clarified that US arms sales to Taiwan take years to process and are unrelated to the ongoing conflict with Iran, following a senior US official suggesting there was a pause due to needing weapons for the Iran conflict.
